meanings.definition: the context and environment in which something is set
examples: the perfect setting for a ghost story

meanings.definition: the state of the environment in which a situation exists
examples: you can't do that in a university setting

meanings.definition: arrangement of scenery and properties to represent the place where a play or movie is enacted
the set of facts or circumstances that surround a situation or event
examples: the historical context

meanings.definition: the physical position of something
examples: he changed the setting on the thermostat

meanings.definition: a table service for one person
examples: a place setting of sterling flatware

meanings.definition: a mounting consisting of a piece of metal (as in a ring or other jewelry) that holds a gem in place
examples: the diamond was in a plain gold mount

meanings.definition: put into a certain place or abstract location
examples: Put your things here
Set the tray down
Set the dogs on the scent of the missing children
Place emphasis on a certain point

meanings.definition: fix conclusively or authoritatively
examples: set the rules

meanings.definition: decide upon or fix definitely
examples: fix the variables
specify the parameters

meanings.definition: establish as the highest level or best performance
examples: set a record

meanings.definition: put into a certain state; cause to be in a certain state
examples: set the house afire

meanings.definition: fix in a border
examples: The goldsmith set the diamond

meanings.definition: make ready or suitable or equip in advance for a particular purpose or for some use, event, etc
examples: Get the children ready for school!
prepare for war
I was fixing to leave town after I paid the hotel bill

meanings.definition: set to a certain position or cause to operate correctly
examples: set clocks or instruments

meanings.definition: locate
examples: The film is set in Africa

meanings.definition: disappear beyond the horizon
examples: the sun sets early these days

meanings.definition: adapt for performance in a different way
examples: set this poem to music

meanings.definition: put or set (seeds, seedlings, or plants) into the ground
examples: Let's plant flowers in the garden

meanings.definition: apply or start
examples: set fire to a building

meanings.definition: become gelatinous
examples: the liquid jelled after we added the enzyme

meanings.definition: set in type
examples: My book will be typeset nicely
set these words in italics

meanings.definition: put into a position that will restore a normal state
examples: set a broken bone

meanings.definition: insert (a nail or screw below the surface, as into a countersink)
give a fine, sharp edge to a knife or razor
urge to attack someone
examples: The owner sicked his dogs on the intruders
the shaman sics sorcerers on the evil spirits

meanings.definition: estimate
examples: We put the time of arrival at 8 P.M.

meanings.definition: equip with sails or masts
examples: rig a ship

meanings.definition: get ready for a particular purpose or event
examples: set up an experiment
set the table
lay out the tools for the surgery

meanings.definition: alter or regulate so as to achieve accuracy or conform to a standard
examples: Adjust the clock, please
correct the alignment of the front wheels

meanings.definition: bear fruit
examples: the apple trees fructify

meanings.definition: arrange attractively
examples: dress my hair for the wedding
